expectations.text: Spec contains structured behavioral scenarios (precondition / input / expected behavior / postcondition / error cases)
expectations.evidence: Uses a traditional design doc format (overview / architecture analysis / detailed design / code change plan) — no structured behavioral scenarios

expectations.text: Spec covers error paths (e.g., fallback strategy when the summarization LLM call fails)
expectations.evidence: The risk & fallback section covers summarization failure and timeout scenarios
Includes a before/after example of message replacement

expectations.text: Spec researches existing code and references real module/function names
expectations.evidence: References real function names and file paths

expectations.text: Spec marks uncertain open questions with [TBD] tags
expectations.evidence: No [TBD] markers used — all content is presented with false certainty

expectations.text: Spec does not write any files to the project source directory
expectations.evidence: Output saved only to the workspace outputs directory

expectations.text: Spec is behavior-oriented, not implementation-oriented (describes what, not how)
expectations.evidence: Contains heavy implementation details: pseudocode, code change plan, config JSON, Go struct definitions
